title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. Acceptance procedures are utilized to provide a further barrier to loss of integrity, as the

Maintenance Plan Varian implements a product lifecycle framework that guides the incorporation of the Secure by Design principle in Varian products and solutions. The lifecycle framework also addresses the continuous management of cybersecurity risks after the products or solutions are released to the field. As part of the post-market cybersecurity maintenance, cybersecurity risk assessments are conducted periodically, or whenever it is needed in response to post-market cybersecurity signals such as 3rd party component vulnerability disclosure (e.g., critical Microsoft vulnerability CVE-2019-0708). Depending on nature and the level of risks, the results of the post-market cybersecurity risk assessments can lead to product updates, bug fixes, field services, Product Advisory, or Product Security Notification.

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

title: 8.0 Production and Post-production Information
text_content: Production and post-production information will be continually monitored and analyzed to determine if new risks have been identified and to review the assessments of known risks to maintain an effective risk management process. This information includes, but is not limited to, design and process changes, revisions of standards, identifications of defects, and reports of complaints. In addition to Varian’s information about the device, the following shall be reviewed: • Information on similar devices on the market; • Publicly available information such as recalls, FDA Medical Device Reports, and vigilance reports; • Scientific literature. Analysis of relevant data is used to demonstrate that risk-based decisions and risk control measures for this device are appropriate and effective. Figure 1 shows the overall V&V planning process starting with the Verification and Validation Plans. From there, multiple Test Design Strategy documents, Test Specifications, Test Reports/Logs, and Trace Matrices may be created. The final V&V results are documented in the Verification and Validation Summary Reports. Verification and Validation Leads create the Verification and Validation Plans and Summary Reports and all groups responsible for verification or validation activities during a project create Test Design Strategy documents, Test Specifications, Test Reports/Logs, and Trace Matrices. title: 5. Risk Management (Continued)
text_content: There are two high-level categorization of post-market cybersecurity incidents. First, there are cybersecurity incidents that involve the possible exploit of potential Varian product vulnerabilities. Secondly, there are incidents that involve the exploit of vulnerabilities in customer operating environment (non-Varian). Varian has both cybersecurity-specific and non-cybersecurity-specific practices in place to support customers and respond to cybersecurity incidents of both categories in the post market phase. The product security team is involved in both practices. The team acts as an interface to identify incidents where both potential customer environment vulnerabilities and potential Varian product vulnerabilities are at play. As an example, there was a cybersecurity incident involving multiple customers in Europe. The customers are all part of a private clinic group and share the IT backbone. One of the customers

source: D78333 Ethos 4.0 Description of interoperability Study Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 1. Basic Information > 2.2 Interoperation Maintenance
text_content: Support & Maintenance Process Customers and any employee that experience erroneous behavior are advised to report these errors to the business unit that has sold the TPS system. These reports are analyzed by the respective complaint handling department. Every error is inserted into the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) and complaint handling procedures and, where necessary, is reproduced in the laboratory for analysis. The complaint handling and the CAPA procedures ensure that the complaints are assessed for the need of Medical Device Reporting (MDR). Also ensured are a root cause analysis of the erroneous behavior and the provision of appropriate change requests for fixing the error. Depending on the significance of the problem, required changes are made by returning to the appropriate development state and implementing appropriate fixes. The changes are then retested and re-evaluated and are either distributed as updates through standard service
